channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Juan Soto's 3-run homer in 10th sends Yankees past Guardians 5-2 and into World Series for 41st time
item.description: Soto hit a three-run homer with two outs in the 10th inning and the Yankees advanced to their 41st World Series — and first in 15 years — by beating the Cleveland Guardians 5-2 in Game 5 of the AL Championship Series on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: SJSU moves to 5-2 in homecoming win over Wyoming
item.description: Walker Eget got his first-career start and finished 20-for-38 with 318 passing yards, one touchdown and two interceptions.
item.dc:creator: Christian Vieyra, The Mercury News

item.title: Alonso and Mets extend season again, beating Dodgers 12-6 to send NLCS back to LA for Game 6
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Not easy to knock out these New York Mets. Pete Alonso golfed an early three-run homer and the Mets hammered an ineffective Jack Flaherty, extending the National League Championship Series with a 12-6 victory over the Los Angeles Dodgers in Game 5 on Friday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Injured Alex Cobb dropped from Guardians' ALCS roster, replaced by Ben Lively
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Pitcher Alex Cobb was dropped from Cleveland's American League Championship Series roster on Tuesday after he got hurt yet again in the opener, leaving the Guardians in search of a Game 5 starter against the New York Yankees. “It’s been a challenging year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Shohei Ohtani stolen base streak ends at 36 in NLCS, caught for first time since July 22
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles Dodgers keep on winning in the playoffs, even without a bunch of home runs and stolen bases by Shohei Ohtani.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Matt McCarty goes from Korn Ferry to the Masters after winning in Utah for first PGA Tour title
item.description: Matt McCarty started the year on the Korn Ferry Tour and now is headed to the Masters, winning his first PGA Tour title Sunday at the Black Desert Championship with a 4-under 67 and one swing that all but sealed it.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Breanna Stewart helps New York even WNBA Finals series as Liberty beat Lynx 80-66 in Game 2
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Breanna Stewart wasn't going to let history repeat itself. With the Minnesota Lynx making another fourth quarter run, the two-time league MVP refused to let the New York Liberty lose again.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Doubs catches 2 TD passes in his return from a suspension to help Packers rout Cardinals 34-13
item.description: Romeo Doubs wanted to make amends after serving a one-game suspension for what the Green Bay Packers described as conduct detrimental to the team.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mayfield passes for 4 TDs as the Bucs treat storm-weary fans to a 51-27 win over the Saints
item.description: Baker Mayfield overcame three turnovers with 325 yards and four touchdowns passing, and the Tampa Bay Buccaneers defeated the New Orleans Saints 51-27 in what they hoped would be a morale boost to their many fans who endured Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Billy Crystal and Spike Lee take their places at the Hall of Fame as basketball superfans
item.description: Honored for his devotion to a basketball team that doesn't have a Hall of Fame history, Billy Crystal couldn't help but note the irony. The actor is being added to the Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame’s James F.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Former LSU pole vaulter Mondo Duplantis proposes to girlfriend during Vogue photo shoot
item.description: Duplantis got down on one knee and proposed to Desiré Inglander while taking photos in The Hamptons. According to Vogue Scandinavia, the couple has been together for four years.
item.dc:creator: Trinity Velazquez, NewsNation

item.title: Bears TE Cole Kmet catches 2 of Caleb Williams' 4 TD passes, handles long snaps in 35-16 rout of Jaguars in London
item.description: WATCH: Jarrett Payton loves what he's seeing from the Bears' offense in the attached video from Monday's WGN Morning News. LONDON (AP) — Chicago Bears tight end Cole Kmet faced his biggest fear in football with no notice. In a snap, you might say.
item.dc:creator: KEN MAGUIRE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: AP Top 25: Oregon, Penn State move behind No. 1 Texas. Army, Navy both ranked for 1st time since '60
item.description: Oregon and Penn State each moved up a spot in The Associated Press college football poll on Sunday following thrilling wins in high-profile games, and Top 25 newcomers Navy and Army are in the rankings together for the first time since 1960.
item.dc:creator: ERIC OLSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ruth Chepngetich of Kenya smashes world record by nearly 2 minutes at Chicago Marathon
item.description: Ruth Chepngetich of Kenya smashed the world record by nearly two minutes at the Chicago Marathon on Sunday, winning in 2:09:56.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bears dominate Jaguars in London, 35-16, to extend winning streak to 3 games going into bye week
item.description: The Bears capped off their London trip in style Sunday, routing the Jacksonville Jaguars, 35-16, at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium to extend their winning streak to three games and improve to 4-2 on the season.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: North Carolina football player dies from rare lung cancer at 23
item.description: UNC had honored Tylee Craft, 23, during Saturday's game. Coach Mack Brown, however, said he hadn't learned of Craft's death until after the game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5
